title: Fe-TiC-H machine learning interatomic potential for predicting hydrogen segregation and diffusion in TiC/Fe
abstract:
Author(s): Saurabh Sagar, Poulumi Dey, and Francesco Maresca
Carbide particles act as effective hydrogen traps that can mitigate hydrogen embrittlement in steels, with their trapping efficacy governed by the binding energy of hydrogen at atomic-scale sites within the carbide and at the carbide/matrix interface. Within the carbide, carbon vacancies are establi…
[Phys. Rev. Materials 10, 063601] Published Fri Jun 05, 2026
relevance_reason: MLIP development for Fe-TiC-H; explicit atoms, potential training, and application to segregation/diffusion, satisfies (a)-(c).

title: Aerobic C–H bond activation at a Pd center under aqueous conditions
abstract:
Chem. Sci., 2026, Advance Article
Dae Young Bae, Nicholas P. Ruhs, Liviu M. Mirica
Facile aerobic C–H activation proceeds at a Pd center under aqueous conditions. Water-assisted hydrogen bonding lowers the activation barrier, and combined experimental and DFT studies identify the C–H bond activation as the rate-determining step.
relevance_reason: DFT study on C-H activation at Pd center; satisfies all criteria (explicit atoms, DFT method, mechanistic contribution).
